# Page 1^page=1
DKE Deutsche Kommission Elektrotechnik Elektronik Informationstechnik Deutsches Mitglied in IEC, CENELEC und ETSI 651.0.12_2026-0005 Detailinformationen Offenbach am Main, 2026-06-23 Classification of electrical connectors by AI { Die Geschäftsstelle übersendet anbei das im Betreff genannte Schriftstück zur Information. gez. Thomas Sentko Referat DKE Deutsche Kommission Elektrotechnik Elektronik Informationstechnik Telefon: +49 69 6308-209 thomas.sentko@vde.com Merianstraße 28; 63069 Offenbach am Main; Telefon: +49 69 6308-0; Telefax: +49 69 6308-9863 E-Mail: dke@vde.com Internet: http://www.dke.de

# Page 2^page=2
https://www.perplexity.ai Here is a practical classification of electrical connectors: By function • Power connectors: carry electrical power. • Signal connectors: carry control or low-level signals. • Data connectors: carry digital data, such as USB or Ethernet types. • RF/coaxial connectors: carry radio-frequency signals. • Hybrid connectors: combine power, signal, and data in one interface. By physical form • Circular connectors: round housings, common in industrial, aerospace, and rugged applications. • Rectangular connectors: used widely in electronics, automation, and rack systems. • PCB connectors: mount on printed circuit boards for board-to-board or wire-to- board links. • Terminal blocks and wire connectors: used for joining wires in installations and panels. By connection method • Plug and socket: classic mateable pair. • Threaded: screw-coupled for secure locking. • Bayonet / quick-release: twist-lock style. • Push-pull: fast mating and unmating. • Crimp / solder / IDC: classified by how the wire is terminated to the contact. By environment and protection • Standard connectors: for normal indoor use. • Waterproof / dustproof connectors: for outdoor or harsh environments. • High-temperature connectors: for hot operating conditions. • EMI/RFI-filtered connectors: reduce electrical noise and interference. • High-voltage connectors: for power transmission at elevated voltages. By application • Industrial connectors • Automotive connectors • Aerospace and military connectors • Consumer electronics connectors • Communication/network connectors • Audio/video connectors

# Page 3^page=3
A simple example is a USB connector, which would be classified as a data connector, usually rectangular, plug-and-socket style, and intended for consumer electronics or communication devices.
